old_claim.note: Leather Working provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct sailing to leather_working dependency remains because the corrected source-backed node is anchored to Naqada II ship-under-sail evidence and square textile/canvas sail architecture, not hide-sail production.

source_shape.source_locator: Sailing ships / Types of sails: describes early sailing ships using a large square canvas suspended from a yard.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source grounds the sail-material architecture in canvas rather than leather or hide processing.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the corrected node is pinned to ship-under-sail evidence and canvas sail architecture, leather_working is an unsupported material-specific prerequisite.

ontology_before: Sailing depended directly on leather_working, laundering a possible hide-sail material into the definition of early sailing.
ontology_after: Sailing is scoped to source-backed sail propulsion and depends on boats, oars, textiles, and cordage without a direct leather-working edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: the direct leather_working dependency is removed from sailing.
would_reject_if: The sailing node is rescoped to a source-backed hide-sail tradition.
A source shows the Naqada II ship-under-sail evidence depended specifically on leather-working.
Removing the edge leaves sailing without any sail-material prerequisite.
short_reason: Leather-working was a possible material path, not a sourced direct prerequisite for early sailing.
